tools.name: ImageDescription
tools.description: A useful tool that returns a brief description of the input image.

tools.name: GoogleSearch
tools.description: A tool that performs a Google search and returns the top k results.

inputs.name: k
inputs.description: The number of top search results to return.

dialogs.content: Identify the organ shown and state whether this anatomical variant is common or rare.

dialogs.thought: First, review the gross specimen to identify the organ and any duplicated or accessory structures.

content.content: Gross specimen photograph showing an elongated sac-like organ consistent with a gallbladder, along with a smaller separate sac-like structure in the foreground, suggesting a duplicated or accessory biliary structure. A scale/ruler is present below the specimen.

dialogs.thought: Use general medical knowledge to determine how common accessory gallbladders are.

arguments.query: accessory gallbladder incidence rare congenital anomaly

content.content: Accessory or duplicated gallbladder is a rare congenital biliary anomaly, reported in a very small percentage of the population and often discovered incidentally during imaging or surgery.

dialogs.content: The organ shown is the gallbladder. The presence of an additional smaller sac-like structure highlights an accessory gallbladder. This is a rare congenital anatomical variant and is usually discovered incidentally during imaging or surgical procedures.
